И тогда, однажды, сам Господь Кришна явился перед этой женщиной и поставил свою ногу на дробильный камень, остановив тем самым ее движения, и попросил ее прекратить измельчать зерна, потому что громкий звук мешает его преданным.Тогда пожилая женщина ответила Господу, что, если она перестанет молоть зерна, то не сможет зарабатывать никаких денег для поддержания своего существования. Господь ответил: “С этой минуты, на твоем дробильном камне проявится отпечаток моей стопы и люди отовсюду будут приходить сюда, чтобы взглянуть на него и будут оставлять небольшие подношения, ты сможешь прожить на эти пожертвования”. Жители Вриндавана по сей день приходят сюда к Джару Мандале получить даршан отпечатка стопы Господа Кришны.
Nice cozy place with a good and available choice of traditional indian cuisine. Here You can get a nice lanch for two only for about 300 rs.
Небольшое уютное кафе с хорошим и доступным выбором блюд национальной индийской кухни. Здесь можно пообедать вдвоем на сумму около трехсот рупий.

When Srila Prabhupada first moved to Vrndavana as a vanaprastha, he stayed in a room on the second floor of this temple from 1956 to 1959, before moving to Radha-Damodara. It is not possible to have darsana of Srila Prabhupada's rooms as they are now occupied by others.

Когда Шрила Прабхупада принял ванапрастху и перебрался во Вриндаван, прежде чем поселиться в храме Радха-Дамодара, он жил в комнате на втором этаже храма с 1956 по 1959 годы. Сейчас получить даршан в его комнатах невозможно, потому что они заселены другими людьми.

Near to the VamSi Gopala Temple is the tooth samadhi of Gadadhara Pandita, who was one of the Panca-tattva, and a very close confident of Lord Chaitanya from childhood. Lord Chaitanya ordered Gadadhara Pandita that he should never leave jagannatha Puri. Gadadhara's disciple, Nayanananda, brought the tooth of Gadadhara to Vrndavana, and placed it this samddhi. 

Nayanananda's samddhi also used to be here but has now disappeared. Gadadhara Pandita was born one year after Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in the year 1487. 

From childhood he always associated with the Lord as his dearmost friend. They both attended the same school and even lived close to one another in Navadvipa. As Gadadhara grew up he was almost inseparable from Lord Chaitanya who was his very life and soul. Later when Lord Chaitanya returned from Gaya, the Lord began to manifest Krishna-prema day and night. He would float in an ocean of love of God and continuously chant the holy names of Krishna. On seen the Lord's ecstatic mood, Gadadhara also became overwhelmed with Krishna-prema, shedding tears of love in divine ecstasy. When Lord Chaitanya was performing His Navadvipa lila it was Gadadhara who was His chief assistant. Another name of Lord Chaitanya is Gadadharaprananatha, the life and soul of Gadadhara. Once when the great devotee Pundarika Vidyanidhi came to Navadvipa, Gadadhara along with Mukunda went to visit him. On seeing Pundarika, who was dressed very opulently like a king, in fine clothes and sitting on silken cushions, Gadadhara began to think that he was just another materialistic person and began to lose faith in his reputation as a devotee. On understanding Gadadhara's thoughts, Mukunda began to sing a verse about Krishna-lila from Srimad Bhagavatam, suddenly Pundarika Vidyanidhi began crying out “Oh, Krishna, Krishna!” Tears flooded his eyes and he feel on the ground and after rolling back and forth a number of times, lost consciousness.

Upon seeing this, Gadadhara felt very bad having criticized Pundarika within his mind, he therefore resolved that in order to become free from an offence against such a mahabhagavata, he would take initation from him. When Pundarika regained consciousness, Gadadhara surrendered at his lotus feet and became his disciple. When Lord Chaitanya took sannyasa and went to Jagganatha Puri, Gadadhara also accepted sannyasa and followed Him. Gadadhara engaged in the service of the Tota Gopinatha Deity and would also speak on Srimad Bhagavatam, Lord Chaitanya would often come and listen to Gadadhara‘s discourses. The Lord ordered Gadadhara never to leave Puri but to remain there always as a Setra sannyasi. When the time came for Lord Chaitanya to end His earthly pastimes He disappeared into the Tota Gopinatha Deity of Gadadhara. They, who in Vrndavana are Radha and Krishna, take the form of Gaura-gadadhara in the pastimes of Lord Chaitanya in Navadvipa. Gadadhara Pandita Gosvami was an incarnation of the pleasure potency and considered non-different from Srimati Radharani, the eternal consort of Lord Sri Krishna.

This is one of the oldest temples of Vrndavana and was completed in 1627. After Emperor Akbar's Visit to Vrndavana in the year 1570, he gave permission for four temples to be built by the Gaudiya Vaisnavas, which were Madana-mohana, Govindaji, Gopinatha and jugal Kis’ore. After the desecration by Aurangzeb's soldiers in 1670, this temple was completely abandoned. It is sometimes called the Keыi ghata temple, as it is located next to this ghata.

Это один из древнейших храмов Вриндавана, его строительство было завершено в 1627 году. После того как Император Акбар посетил Вриндаван в 1570 году, он дал свое разрешение на строительство четырех Гаудия-вайшнавских храмов, которыми стали храмы Мадана-Мохана, Говиндаджи, Гопинатха и Югала Кишора. После осквернения солдатами Аурангзеба в 1670 году этот храм был полностью оставлен. Его иногда называют храмом Кеши-гхата, так как он расположен неподалеку от этого гата..

This is the place where Lord Krishna killed the Kesi demon who appeared in the form of gigantic horse. This is also the most famous bathing place in Vrndavana. An arati to Yamuna Devi is held here every evening. This Kesi demon was another friend of the evil king Kamsa. By this demon‘s mystic powers, he assumed the form of a giant horse, and in a wild and angry mood, entered Vrndavana where he found Lord Krishna playing by the bank of the Yamuna river with His cowherd boyfriends. 

Lord Krishna heard the frighteningly loud whinnying sounds of the demon horse, which caused terror to all the inhabitants of Vrndavana. Krishna then came before the Kesi demon to accept his challenge and immediately, Kesi started galloping toward Krishna, hoping to trample Him to death. The demon did not know that this small child, enjoying pastimes with His boyhood friends on the bank of the Yamuna, was non-other than the all powerful, Supreme Personality of Godhead. As the horse demon approached, Krishna caught hold of his legs and then circled the demon a number of times. When the demon was completely baffled as to what was happening Krishna picked him up, just as a child picks up a flower, and threw him one hundred yards away which knocked him unconscious.

On regaining consciousness, Kesi again charged towards Krishna, this time Lord Krishna pushed His hand inside the horse‘s mouth, knocking out all his teeth. The Lord's hand then began to expand in the horse‘s throat. Kesi demon immediately started choking to death. His legs started shaking, sweat poured out from his body, his eye balls bulged out from their sockets and as he died, he passed stool and urine simultaneously. Thus, another terrible demon was eliminated from the face of the earth by the grace of Lord Sri Krishna. After killing the Kesi demon, Krishna took His bath at this very same ghata.

This Deity was worshipped by Madhu Pandita Gosvami, one of the close associates of Lord Chaitanya. The Gopinatha Deity was originally installed by Vajranaba, Lord Krishna‘s grandson, 5,000 years ago. The Deity was rediscovered at VamSi-vata by Paramananda Bhattacarya, who gave the Deity to Madhu Pandita. This temple was also desecrated by the soldiers of Aurangzeb in 1670 and a new temple was constructed just behind the old one. The original Gopinatha Deity is in Jaipur, where He was sent for safe keeping. The Deity in this temple is the pratibha-murti (replica) which is considered to be non-different from the original Deity.

There is a very nice story of how Ananga Manjari appeared on the altar at the Gopinatha temple. Normally in all temples Radharani stands on the right of Krishna, but in this temple she stands on the left and Ananga Manjari stands on the right. 

In the late 16th century when Lord Nityananda's eternal consort Janava Mata visited Vrndavana, she felt that the Deity of Radharani at the Gopinatha temple was too small. On her return to Bengal she had a new Deity of Radharani carved and sent it to Vrndavana. This new Deity of Radharani was than installed on the right side of Lord Gopinatha and the smaller one moved to the left side. 

It so happened that all the devotees visiting the Gopinatha temple concluded that the new Deity looked more like Janava Mata than Radharani. This was because the new Deity was carved by one of Janava Mata's dearmost disciples. The devotees soon came to realize that the newly installed Deity was in fact Ananga Manjari, Radharani's younger sister, of whom Ma Janava is a direct expansion. Some devotees felt that out of her immense love for her younger sister, Radharani consented to allow Ananga Manjari to stand in Her own place next to Lord Gopinatha, allowing her the chance of serving the Divine Couple to her hearts content. It would appear that Ananga Manjari has her very own transcendental way of appearing on the altar so she may eternally render loving service to the lotus feet of Sri Sri Radha Gopinatha. 

Another very interesting point is that when Lord Balarama, Krishna's elder brother, desires to take part in the amourous pastimes of conjugal love between Radha and Krishna and the gopis, the expands Himself and becomes non-other than Ananga Manjari, the younger sister Radharani.

Situated in the compound of the Gopinatha temple is the samadhi of Lord Chaitanya's associate, Madhu Pandita Gosvam‘i, who was a disciple of Gadadhara Pandita. Madhu Pandita established the temple of Radha Gopinatha and lived in Vrndavana until he entered samadhi, his sacred body was then entombed at this spot just near the original main gate of the Gopinatha temple.

Неподалеку от храма Гопинатха находится самадхи святого и близкого спутника Господа Чайтаньи, Мадху Пандита Госвами, который был учеником Гададхара Пандита. Мадху Пандит жил во Вриндаване до тех пор пока не вошел в самадхи, он основал храм Радха Гопинатха и именно у первоначальных ворот храма было погребено его священное тело.

These are the celebrated Deities worshipped by Jagannatha dasa Babaji when he was staying at Surya-kunda. They were moved to Vrndavana when a gang of theives tried to steal them having mistaken them to be pure gold (sona) because of their brilliant shine. When Jagannatha dasa Babaji returned to Bengal these beautiful Deities were looked after by a local brahmana.

Это знаменитые Божества, которым поклонялся Джаганнатха дас Бабаджи, когда он жил у Сурья-кунды. Их перевезли во Вриндаван, когда бандиты попыталась украсть их, решив, что они выполнены из чистого золота (сона) из-за их ослепительного блеска. Когда Джаганнатха дас Бабаджи вернулся в Бенгалию, за этими прекрасными Божествами ухаживали местные брахманы.

This famouos saint of Vrndavana is said to have lvied here for about 700 years, performing bhajana at Brahma-kunda. Bilvamangala is also well known for his devotional songs about Lord Krishna. He was the most prominent disciple and follower of Vishnu Swami from South India and moved to Vrndavana around 700 years. He is also known by the name of Lila suka.

Этот знаменитый святой Вриндавана, как говорится, жил здесь около семисот лет назад, совершая свое поклонение на Брахма-кунде. Так же Билвамангала известен своими песнями о Господе Кришне, исполненными глубокой преданности. Он был одним из самых преданных учеников Вишну Свами из Южной Индии и переехал во Вриндаван около 700 лет назад. Он так же известен под именем Лила Шука.

This temple is dedicated to Lord Chaitanya. The Deity is over seven feet tall and is well known throughout Vrindavana as ‘Amiya-Nimai.’ ‘Amiya’ means ambrosia as well as immortal. This Deity was installed by the Gosvamis of the Radharamana Temple.

Этот храм посвящен Господу Чайтанье. Божество более семи футов в высоту известно во всем Вриндаване как Амия-Нимай. "Амия" означает амброзию, или даже бессмертие. Это Божество было установлено Госвами храма Радхарамана.

This temple is dedicated to Lord Chaitanya. The Deity is over seven feet tall and is well known throughout Vrindavana as ‘Amiya-Nimai.’ ‘Amiya’ means ambrosia as well as immortal. This Deity was installed by the Gosvamis of the Radharamana Temple.

Этот храм посвящен Господу Чайтанье. Божество более семи футов в высоту известно во всем Вриндаване как Амия-Нимай. "Амия" означает амброзию, или даже бессмертие. Это Божество было установлено Госвами храма Радхарамана.

Inside this courtyard to the left is the temple of Radha-madhava. The small silver Deity of Krishna on the bottom shelf is Jula Thakura, the Deity of Srinivasa Achrya, who used to keep Him in his bead bag (jula).

Внутри этого дворика, слева, находится храм Радха-мадхавы. Внизу на алтаре стоит маленькое серебряное Божество Кришны - Джула Тхакура, Божество Шриниваса Ачарьи, который держал его в бисерной сумочке - джула.

A disciple of Gopala Bhatta Gosvami. Srinivasa Acarya was also an intimate student of Srila Jiva Gosvami, Who ordered him to preach Lord Chaitanya’s message in Bengal. The samadhi is in a room on the right side of the courtyard. Srinivasa had many disciples but Ramacandra Kaviraja was most prominent. Srinivasa wasa a great preacher of Bhagavata, and even converted King Birhambir, the dacoit king, to Vaisnavism.

The father of Srinivasa attended the sannyasa ceremony of Lord Chaitanya in Katwa, and accompanied by his wife, he again met Lord Chaitanya in Puri where the Lord predicted that they would have a son named SriniVaa. As a youth, Srinivasa became known as a most brilliant student, he had such beautifull bodily features and golden lustre that he reminded everyone of Lord Chaitanya. It is said that Srinivasa was the embodiment of Goura-shakti, Lord Chaitanya‘s spiritual potency, and was in fact a one-sixteenth part of the full spirit of the Lord. 

While on his way to Puri to meet Lord Chaitanya for the first time, he learned of the Lord's untimely departure from the world, which left him in an ocean of sorrow. On arriving in Puri he took shelter of Gadadhara Pandita who informed him of Lord Chaitanya’s order that he should learn Srimad Bhagavatam from him, and should later go to Vrndavana, take initiation from Gopala Bhatta Gosvami and study bhakti-sastras. After Gadadhara Pandit's disappearance, Srinivasa went to Vrndavana where he took initation from Gopala

Bhatta Gosvami and studied under Jiva Gosvami, along with Narottama dasa and Syamananda. After completing his studies, Srinivasa was ordered by Jiva Gosvami to go to Bengal by bullock cart with Narottama and Syamananda, and distribute the books of the Gosvamis, thus becoming the first book distribution party in the history of Gaudiya Vaisnavism. Unfortunately, disaster was to strike, on the way to Bengal all the books were stolen by a gang of thieves sent by the dacoit king, Birhmabir of Bana Vishnupura, whose astrologers had informed him of a most valuable treasure being carried by Srinivasa and his party. After recovering from the shock of losing all the books, Srinivasa asked Narottama and Syamananda to continue the journey while Srinivasa himself stayed behind and searched for the books. Srinivasa just happened to visit the court at Vana Vishnupura where he heard an unbona-fide discourse by the court pandita on Srimad Bhagavatam, Srinivasa challenged by the court pandita who sarcastically requested Srinivasa to give a better discourse, which Srinivasa did, referring to the commentaries of the Gosvamis of Vrndavana. All those who heard Srinivasa's Bhagavatam discourse were astounded, including the king who was sitting in the audience. The king immediately surrendered to Srinivasa and asked him if he could do any service for him, Srinivasa requested the king to help him find the sacred books of the Gosvamis that had been stolen by thieves. The king suddenly broke down and started crying, condemning himself as the most sinful wretch for having ordered his men to steal the sacred books, thinking them to be valuable treasure. The king showed the books to Srinivasa who became ecstatic at the sudden good fortune. King Birhambir of Vana Vishnupur later became a disciple of Srinivasa Acarya receiving the name Chaitanya dasa, he converted his whole kingdom into a Vaisnava state. 

Srinivasa Acarya became one of the most important preachers of Lord Chaitanya's movement and along with Narottama dasa Ihakura and Syamananda Pandita inundated the whole of Bengal, Orissa, Assam and Manipura with Krishna bhakti and Hari nama sankirtan. Srinivasa not only became famous as a preacher of Bhagavatam but was also known for his wonderful kirtans. He wrote the famous song glorifying the six Gosvamis of Vrndavana known as Sat-gosvamiastakam. 

He later married at the request of his mother, his daughter Hemlata became a great preacher in the sankirtana movement. Srinivasa made hundred of disciple but Ramacandra Kaviraja, the close friend of Narottama dasa. Thakur was the most prominent. Srinivasa Acarya became one of the most important preachers in the generation immediately following Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. 

’ Srinivasa Acarya was an incarnation of Mani manjari, one of the intimate maidservants of Srimati Radharani in the spiritual world.

A disciple of Srinivasa Acarya and close friend of Narottama dasa, he was one of the eight Kavirajas and his samadhi is next to the samadhi of Srinivasa. He received the title Kaviraja (king of poets) from the Gosvamis who were very pleased by his wonderful poetry about the pastimes of Lord Krishna and Lord Chaitanya. He assisted Narottama in organising the great Kheturi festival celebrating the appearance day of Lord Chaitanya. He also had a brother named Govinda Kaviraja, who was another of the celebrated eight Kavirajas. He finally disappeared from the world in Vrndavana and his samаdhi was placed next to his Spiritual master at Srinivasa Acarya Kunja.

Он был учеником Шриниваса Ачарьи и близким другом Нароттама даса, одним из восьми Кавираджей, его самадхи находится рядом с его учителем Шринивасом. Он получил титул Кавираджа (король поэтов) от Госвами, которые были очень довольны его замечательной поэзией о трансцендентных играх Господа Кришны и Господа Чайтаньи. Он помогал Нароттаме в организации великого фестиваля Хетури, в честь дня явления Господа Чайтаньи. У него также был брат по имени Говинда Кавираджа, который так же был одним из восьми знаменитых кавираджей. Он оставил мир во Вриндаване, и его самадхи было помещено рядом с его духовным учителем в Шриниваса Ачарья Кунже.
This is the daughter of Srinivasa Acarya and as a powerful preacher of VaisnaVism made many disciples, her smadhi is near the samadhi of her father.
Это дочь Шриниваса Ачарьи и очень сильная проповедница, у которой было множество последователей, ее самадхи находится рядом с самадхи ее отца.

These are the Deities of Gopala Guru Gosvami. This temple was established by Gopala Guru Gosvami who was a disciple of Vakresvara Pandita. As a child, Gopala used to meet Lord Chaitanya. Once, the Lord jokingly called him guru, the name stuck and he became known as Gopala Guru. He studied bhakti-sastras under both Svarupa Damodara and Raghunatha dasa Gosvami.

Здесь находятся Божества Гопалы Гуру Госвами. Этот храм был установлен Гопалом Гуру Госвами, который был учеником Вакрешвары Пандита. В детстве Гопала встречал Господа Чайтанью. Однажды Господь в шутку назвал его гуру, имя привязалось к нему, и он стал известен как Гуру Гопала. Он изучал бхакти-шастры, как у Сварупа Дамодара, так и у Рагхунатхи даса Госвами.

Even as a young boy he became a close associate of Lord Chaitanya and was accepted as a disciple by Vakresvara Pandita, he established the temple of Radha Kanta where his Puspa-samatdhi is situated in the temple courtyard. After his disappearance in Jagannatha Puri, his followers who attended the samadhi ceremony there, came back to Vrndavana, and to their surprise, found the very same Gopala Guru Gosvami sitting under the tree in the courtyard of this temple. He informed his followers that the body they saw was his spiritual body. He met all the devotees of Vrndavana and then disppeared while sitting under the same tree. His followers then constructed another smadhi next to this tree.

Еще будучи маленьким мальчиком, он стал близким спутником Господа Чайтаньи и  Вакрешвар Пандит принял его в ученики, позже он основал храм Радха-Канта, где, во внутреннем дворе храма, находится его пушпа-самадхи. После его ухода в Джаганнатхе Пури, его последователи, которые присутствовали на церемонии самадхи, вернулись во Вриндаван и, к их удивлению, нашли Гопала Гуру Госвами, сидящего под деревом во дворе этого храма. Он сообщил своим последователям, что тело, которое они видели, было его духовным телом. Он встретился со всеми преданными Вриндавана, а затем оставил тело, сидя под тем же деревом. Затем его последователи устроили еще одно смадхи рядом с этим самым деревом.

These are the Deities of Radha Syama-raya, belonging to Gauridasa Pandita, who established this temple. He was a very dear follower of both Lord Chaitanya and Lord Nityananda. 
Dhira Samira Ghata means the ghata Where the breeze blows very gently, and gives pleasure to Radha and Krishna, who enjoyed transcendental pastimes in the gardens that were once situated on the banks of the Yamuna at this place. The gardens and ghata have since disappeared in the course of time and the Yamuna river bed has shifted further away.

Здесь установлены Божества Радха Шяма-Рая, принадлежащие Гауридасу Пандиту, который основал этот храм. Он был очень дорогим последователем как Господа Чайтаньи, так и Господа Нитьянанды.
Дхира Самира Гата означает гат, где ветерок очень мягко дует, и доставляет удовольствие Радхе и Кришне, которые наслаждаются трансцендентными играми в садах, раскинувшихся здесь на берегу Ямуны. С тех пор сады и гат исчезли повинуясь неумолимому течению времени, и русло реки Ямуны тоже сдвинулось дальше.
Radha Syama Raya Mandira (Dhira Samira Mandir)
Радха Шьяма Рая Мандир (Дхира Самира Мандир)

Entombed here is the sacred body of Gauridasa Pandita, a direct disciple of Lord Nityananda and close associate of Sri Chaitanya. At this place near Dhira Samira Ghata, Gauridasa performed his bhajana and also established the temple of Radha Syama-raya. Gauridasa lived in Ambika~Kalna, not far from Shantipura. His elder brother had two daughters, Vasudha andJahnava, who were both married to Lord Nityananda. When Lord Chaitanya was going to take Sannyasa, he Visited Gauridasa to say goodbye. Gauridasa was so overcome with feeling of separation, that weeping, he fell at the feet of Lord Chaitanya and Lord Nityananda, and begged Them to never leave his house, otherwise he felt he would die. Seeing the great anxiety of Gauridasa, Lord Chaitanya told him that, “We two brothers will always stay there in your home”. Consoling him with these words, the two brothers Lord Chaitanya and Lord Nityananda, manifested themselves in the form of two perfect Deities, exact replicas of GauraNityananda, Gauridasa was stunned. The Lord informed him, “These two will stay in your home forever and your will internally realize We are always here and will accept all the offerings you cook.” Hearing this, Gauridasa immediately began to cook a wonderful offering. After enjoying the Wonderful meal, Lord Chaitanya and Lord Nityananda left, living behind the two Deities of Themselves which Gauridasa continued to serve With great devotion. Once when a big festival celebrating Gaura-Purnima was being held nearby, these same Gaura-nitai Deities jumped down from the altar joined the kirtan, dancing wildly in ecstasy. Gauridasa had to chase after. Them and when Gaura-nitai saw him coming in an angry mood carrying a big stick. They suddenly disappeared into the heart of Gauridasa's disciple Chaitanya dasa. On seeing this, Gauridasa ' immediately embraced his disciple and from that time on his disciple became known as Hrdaya Chaitanya later became the guru of Syamananda Pandita. Gauridasa Pandita was an incarnation of the cowherd boy Subala, one of the twelve most prominent cowherd boyfriends of Lord Krishna in the spiritual world.

This temple, dedicated to Lord Siva, is very important, as Lord Siva not only protects the holy dhama, but also guards the rasa-mandala. The Siva Linga is 5,000 years old and was originally installed by Lord Krishna’s grandson, Vajranaba. 

Once, during a performance of the rasa-lila, Lord Siva came to see the wonderful dancing of Lord Krishna and the gopis, but when he tried to enter the rasa-mandala, he was stopped by Lalita and Visakha Sakhis, who informed him that as he was a male, he could not enter. Lalita then told Lord Siva to first bathe in the Radharani Manasarovar lake. Lord Siva then went and took bath at this lake and immediately turned into the form of a gopi. He then returned to the rasa-mandala where Lord Krishna welcomed him and gave him the name Gopisvara. Lord Krishna also gave Siva the boon that without Lord Siva's permission, no-one could enter into the rasa-mandala.

Этот храм, посвященный Господу Шиве, очень важен, поскольку Господь Шива не только защищает святую дхаму, но и охраняет раса-мандалу. Этому Шива Лингаму 5000 лет, изначально он был установлен внуком Господа Кришны, Ваджранабой.

Однажды, во время раса-лилы, Господь Шива захотел присоединиться к прекрасным танцам Господа Кришны и гопи, но когда он попытался войти в раса-мандалу, его остановили Лалита и Вишакха Сакхи, которые сообщили ему, что поскольку он мужчина, он не может войти. Затем Лалита сказала Гсподу Шиве, чтобы он искупался в озере Радхарани - Манасаровар. Тогда Господь Шива подчинился и принял омовение в этом озере и тотчас принял в форму гопи. Затем он вновь вернулся к раса-мандале, где Господь Кришна сам принял его и дал ему имя Гопишвара. Господь Кришна также дал Шиве благословение, что без разрешения Господа Шивы никто не мог войти в раса-мандалу.

This is the famous place mentioned in innumerable songs where Lord Krishna came on the beautiful full moon night of the samt season (Autumn), and called all the gopis of Vrndavana to dance with Him, simply by playing an enchanting melody on His transcendental flute. VamSi means flute and vata means Banyan tree. 

There used to be a Banyan tree standing here from the time of Lord Krishna, but about 450 years ago, the Yamuna flooded and washed the tree away. Paramananda Gosvami collected one of the branches and planted it at the same spot. While planting the branch, he discovered the Deity of Gopinatha. This Deity was originally installed 5,000 years ago by Vajranaba, the grandson of Lord Krishna. 

The tree he planted is still there today.

Это очень известное место, упомянуто в бесчисленных песнях, о том как Господь Кришна пришел в прекрасную ночь полнолуния в сезон саттов (Осень) и призвал всех гопи Вриндавана танцевать с Ним, просто сыграв очаровательную мелодию на Своей  трансцендентной флейте. Вамши означает флейта, а вата - дерево Баньяна.

Раньше здесь было дерево Баньяна со времен Господа Кришны, но около 450 лет назад Ямуна разлилась, вышла из берегов и вымыла дерево. Парамананда Госвами сберег одну из ветвей и посадил ее на том же месте. Сажая ветку, он обнаружил Божество Гопинатхи. Это Божество было установлено 5000 лет назад Ваджранабой, внуком Господа Кришны.

Дерево, которое он посадил, находится здесь и по сей день.
This beautifully ornate temple is dedicated to Radha and Krishna and was built by the  Bihari Queen, Indrajit Kunver in 1871.
Этот восхительно украшеный храм посвященный Радхе и Кришне был построен Королевой Бихари, Индраджит Кунвер в 1871 году.

About two hundred years ago, Haridasa, a Ramanandi Vaisnava who was devoted to Lord Krishna, used to meditate on the bank of the Yamuna near VamSi Vata. One night in a dream Lord Jagganatha appeared and informed Haridasa that He would very much like to come and live near the bank of the Yamuna in Vrndavana, and accept the worship of Haridasa. Lord Jagganatha asked Haridasa to immediately go to Jagganatha Puri and as this was the time once every twelve years when the body of the Jagganatha Deity is changed, Haridasa should bring the old body of Lord Jagganatha back to Vrndavana. 

On waking Haridasa gathered all his disciples and with a rousing kirtan set off on foot for Jagganatha Puri. On arriving in Puri, Haridasa went straight to the jagganagtha temple and requested the priests to allow him to take the old body of Lord Jagganatha back to Vrndavana. The priests however flatly refused and advised Haridasa to speak directly to the king of Puri, who was the chief administrator of the temple. On hearing the request of Haridasa, the king also refused and informed Haridasa that the old body of Jagganatha would be put into samadhi as per the ancient custom and no one was allowed to take possession of the old Deity. 

On hearing the king's refusal, Haridasa decided to go the beach and fast until death. The night the king had a dream in which Lord Jagganatha came before him in a very angry mood and demanded to know why the king had refused to follow the order given by the Lord Himself to His servant Haridasa. The king immediately upon waking called for Haridasa, informed him of the dream and granted permission for Haridasa to take the old Deity of Lord Jagganatha back to Vrndavana. The Deities of their Lordships Jagganatha, Subhadra and Baladeva were then placed on a chariot and accompanied by priests and a large contingent of soldiers. Brought to Vrndavana and Installed on the bank of the Yamuna at this same place, which then became known as Jagganatha Ghata.

At this spot underneath the Banyan tree (Vata), Uddhava discussed topics about Lord Krishna's pastimes with Vidura. Uddhava told Vidura that Krishna spoke of no devotee prior to His disappearance except Vidura, whom He mentioned three times over. This brought torrents of tears to Vidura's eyes when he thought of the Lords mercy upon him. Also at this place, Prayag Raja Tirtha (Allahabad) came here in the form of a black horse in order to take bath and relieve himself of all the sins left by pilgrims who visit Prayag. After taking bath at this place, Prayaпs black form turned white, having been freed from all the sins. Brajbasis take bath at this same spot when the Yamuna floods during the monsoon.

На этом месте под деревом Баньяна (Вата) Уддхава обсуждал игры Господа Кришны с Видурой. Уддхава рассказал Видуре, что Кришна не говорил ни об одном преданном перед Своим уходом, кроме Видуры, о котором Он упомянул три раза. Это заставило Видуру разрыдаться, когда он подумал о милости Господа, проявленной к нему. Также в это место Праяг Раджа Тиртха (Аллахабад) пришел в облике черного коня, чтобы принять омовение и освободить себя от всех грехов, оставленных паломниками, которые посещают Праяг. После купания в этом месте Прайага стал белым, освободившись от всех грехов. Браджабаси принимают омовение в этом же месте, когда Ямуна выходит из берегов во время муссонов.

This Deity was installed by Narottama Dasa Thakura at the Kheturi festival in West Bengal, Srinivasa Acarya performed the abhiseka. All the important Vaisnavas of the day, attended the first ever festival to celebrate the appearance day of Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Of the five Deities installed at the time, this is the only one to have been brought to Vrndavana for worship.

Это Божество было установлено Нароттамом Дасом Тхакуром во время празднования в Хетури в Западной Бенгалии, а Шриниваса Ачарья исполнил абхишеку. Все почтенные вайшнавы того времени присутствовали на том первом фестивале, посвященном празднику явления Господа Чайтаньи Махапрабху. Из пяти Божеств, установленных в то время, это единственное, что было принесено во Вриндаван для поклонения.

This is the temple of Lord Krishna Candrama, built in 1810 by a millionaire from Bengal of the name Krishna Candra Sinh, better known as Lala Babu. This is a large temple set in a nice garden, surrounded by a high wall. Lala Babu renounced the world at an early age and became a vairagi. He also constructed the ghatas surrounding Radha-kunda.

Это храм Господа Кришны Кандрамы, построенный в 1810 году миллионером из Бенгалии с именем Кришна Кандра Синх, более известным как Лала Бабу. Это большой храмовый комплекс, расположенный в невероятно красивом саду, окруженном высокой стеной. Лала Бабу в очень раннем возрасте отрекся от мира и стал вайраги. Он же и построил гхат, окружающий ныне Радха-кунду.

This is where Brahma offered his prayers to Lord Krishna after he had stolen all the cowherd boys and calves. Bilvamangala Ihakura performed bhajana here at Brahma Kunda for 700 years. Rupa Gosvami also discovered the Deity of Vrndadevi on the northern side of this kunda. 

This kunda (pool) was created from the tears of Lord Brahma, the secondary creator of the universe, when he tearfully offered his wonderful prayers to Lord Krishna. It is happened that Brahmaji had been hearing about the wonderful activities of Krishna, the cowherd boy of Vrndavana, and suspecting that the might be the Supreme Personality of Godhead, decided to test the Lord by playing a trick. One day, while Lord Krishna was taking His lunch along with the cowherd boys, the calves, who were grazing nearby, were taken away by Lord Brahma. Krishna volunteered to go and see what had happened to the calves and while He was gone, Brahma came and took away all the cowherd boys. By his mystic powers, he hid both the cowherd boys and the calves in a cave. After putting them all into a deep sleep, he then left for his own planet. 

On not finding the calves, Lord Krishna returned and seeing that the cowherd boys were also missing, could understand that this was the trick of the demigod Brahma. As it was time to return home, and He could not return alone, Lord Krishna decided to expand Himself as the calves and cowherd boys. On reaching the village, nobody could understand that the boys and the calves were missing. Lord Krishna had expanded Himself to appear like each and every boy, so much so, that the parents of those boys could not understand that this was not their original child. 
After Lord Brahma had left, he had see and thoughts about his mischief making, and therefore decided to return in case Krishna had become angry and was going to punish him for his impudence. Even though for Brahma, only a few moments passed of his time, here on earth, one whole year had gone by. On arriving back at the scene, Brahma was astonished to see all the same cowherd boys and calves playing with Krishna, and became even more astonished when all the cowherd boys and calves suddenly transformed into four armed forms of Vishnu. Brahma could then understand the omnipotency of Lord Krishna which made Brahma's own display of mystic power look insignificant. Brahma then immediately brought all the cowherd boys and calves stolen by him and returned them to the same spot. He then fell on the ground just like a stick and prayed to Lord Krishna to forgive him for his impudence in trying to test the power of the Lord.
At this time, while offering his prayers, Brahma shed torrents of tears which formed a small lake and became known as the Brahma-kunda. Until a few years ago, this kunda was full of water but at present it has dried up and also fallen into neglect. The Manas Mandir temple overlooks this kunda and by climbing to the rooftop, one not only gets a good View of Brahm kunda but also a wonderful panoramic View of the whole of Vrndavana.

This South Indian style temple was built by the wealthy Seth family of Mathura in the year 1851, and is dedicated to Lord Sri Ranganatha or Rangaji form of Lord Vishnu lying down on the Sesa Naga (celestial serpent). This temple has a traditional South Indian gopuram (gateway) and is surrounded by high walls. It is one of Vrndavana's largest temples. Once a year a grand car festival (Ratha Yatra) is held known as Brahmotsava, during the month of Chait (March April), this festival lasts for 10 days. The Car (Rath) is pulled to the Rangaji Bag gardens nearby. 
This happens to her the only temple in Vrndavana that does not allow foreigners to enter.* in fact, it is one of the few temples in all of India that Still follows this outdated rule. The original Rangaji Mandir is situated at Trichy in Tamil Nadu, and allows entry of all foreigners. 
*(Foreigners can enter into the courtyard and walk around the outside perimeter of the temple. upon paying a small fee.)

Этот храм в южно-индийском стиле был построен богатой семьей Сетта Матхуры в 1851 году и посвящен Господу Шри Ранганатхе, или форме Господа Вишну, лежащего на Сеса-Наге (небесном змее) - Рангаджи. Этот храм оснащен традиционным южноиндийский гопурамом (шлюзом) и окружен высокими стенами. Это один из самых крупных храмов Вриндавана. Один раз в год, в месяц Чаит (март апрель) проходит фестиваль большой колесницы (ратха ятра), известный как Брахмоцава, этот фестиваль длится 10 дней. Колесницу (Ратх) тянут к садам Рангаджи  Баг.
Так получилось, что это единственный храм во Вриндаване, в который не допускают иностранцев. Фактически, это один из немногих храмов оставшихся во всей Индии, который все еще следует этому устаревшему правилу. Оригинальный Рангаджи Мандир расположен в Трихите в Тамилнаде и вход в него открыт всем, даже иностранцам.
* (Иностранцы могут войти во внутренний двор и пройти по внешнему периметру храма, заплатив небольшую плату).

This Deity was established by Srila Rupa Gosvami who was ordered by Lord Chaitanya to excavate all the lost holy places of Vrndavana. Five thousand years earlier, Lord Krishna's grandson, Vajranaba, installed a number of important Deities in Vrndavana, Govindaji was one of these Deities. Rupa Gosvami searched all over Vrndavana to locate the Yogapith, the resting place of the original Deity of Govindaji, but after months of searching, the Deity could not be found. Rupa Gosvami, feeling disappointed, came and sat by the bank of the Yamuna and shedding tears, began calling the name of Lord Chaitanya, Ha Chaitanya, Ha Chaitanya” (Oh Chaitanya). Suddenly, Lord Krishna in the guise of a beautiful brijvasi boy appeared and informed Rupa Gosvami that a cow came every day to a nearby hillock, and emptied its milk into a hole on the hilltop. He asked Rupa to accompany him to the place and see what might be in the hole. Rupa Gosvami followed the boy and upon reaching the drenched hole, Rupa Gosvami immediately fell unconscious in a wave of ecstatic emetions 

When he recovered, he summoned the local people Who excavated the hole and found the beautiful Deity of Lord Govinda. The loud cries of “He Govinda, he Krishna” suddenly reverberated throughout the sky. 

The construction of the temple of Radha Govinda began under the guidance of Raghunatha, Bhatta Gosvami and his disciples headed by Raja Man Singh of jaipur some time after 1570. It was the most impressive edifice that Hindu art had ever produced in the whole of Northern India It towered into the sky seven stories high and was built on top of the hill where the Deity was first discovered, making it the tallest building in this part of India. The temple was completed in 1590. 

Less than one hundred years later, Emperor Aurangzeb, the Muslim zealot, was standing on the ramparts of his fort at Agra, and noticed a bright light burning in the far distance. Upon being informed that this was a large ghee lamp kept atop the Radha Gm mda temple in Vrndavana, he became furious that a Hindu shrine was taller than any Muslim building and immediately ordered destruction. By the time the soldiers arrived, Lord Govindadeva and all the important Deities of Vrndavana had been moved to safer places. The soldiers of Aurangzeb dismantled the top floors of the Govindaji temple and desecrated the sanctum sanatorium as well as mutilated all the stone carvings within the temple.

This is the very spot where Srila Rupa Gosvami found the Govindaji Deity. A special chapel was built to the left side of the main alter and a small Deity of Yogamaya was installed here along with the lotus footprints of Lord Govinda. This Deity of Yogamaya (Lord Krishna's sister) was also worshipped by Rupa Gosvami. During the attack by Aurangzeb on Vrndavana, the Deity of Yogamaya was hidden and later reinstalled in the same chape. One has to ask the pujari (priest) for a darshan of the Yogapitha.

Это то место, где Шрила Рупа Госвами нашел Божество Говиндаджи. С левой стороны от главного алтаря, был построен специальный небольшой храм и было установлено маленькое Божество Йогамайи вместе с отечатком лотосной стопы Господа Говинды. Это Божество Йогамайи (сестры Господа Кришны) также почиталось Рупой Госвами. Во время нападения Аурангзеба на Вриндаван, Божество Йогамайи спрятали, а затем переустановили в том же месте. Чтобы получить даршан  Йогапиты, нужно спросить пуджари (священника).

In the far right hand corner of the 64 samadhi‘s garden, there used to be a pond which is now dry called Venu Kupa, where Lord Krishna dug His flute (venu) into the ground to produce a fresh water spring to quench the thirst of the cowherd boys. In this particular garden are the full samadhis of Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvarni, Subuddhi Raya, and KaSiSVara Pandita. Also here are the samadhis of 64 important followers (Mahants) of Lord Chaitanya. Besides the three full samadhis, the rest of the samadhis are either nama, puspa or smrta samc‘idhis, including the Gosvamis-Rupa, Sanatana, Raghunatha dasa, Gopala Bhatta, Jiva, Lokanatha and Krishnadasa Kaviraja as well as Svarupa Damodara, Candraéekhara Acarya, Ramananda Raya, Sivananda Sena, KaSi Misra, Sikhi Mahiti, Kavi Karnapura, Vasudeva Ghosh, Raghava Pandita, Murari Chaitanya, Chota Haridasa, Jahnava Mata, Kolaveca Sridhara, Mukunda dasa, Saranga, Thakura, Kala Krishna dasa, and others.

Entombed here in the 64 samc‘zdhis garden are the ashes of Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami. It was decided to cremate his body so that the Muslim fanatics could not descreate it. This was also done to Raghunatha dasa Gosvami, who's samcidhi is Radha-kunda. Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami also performed his bhajana in this very same garden. Raghunatha Bhatta, as a child first met Lord Chaitanya in the house of his father Tapan Mishra, who originally hailed from East Bengal but had subsequently moved to Benaras (Kasi). On the way to Vrndavana Lord Chaitanya stopped in Benaras to meet Tapan Mishra and also Candrasekhara Acarya. On the Lord's return from Vrndavana He also stayed in Benaras for ten days. Lord Chaitanya stayed at the house of Candrasekhara and took his meals at the house of Tapan Mishra. Raghunatha Bhatta, although just a child would serve the Lord by massaging His lotus feet. When Raghunatha Bhatta finished his studies he went to Jagganatha Puri to meet  Lord Chaitanya who was very pleased to see him. The Lord ordered Raghunatha Bhatta to remain a lifelong brahmacari. Raghunatha remained at Puri for eight months, he was a very expert cook and would sometimes prepare prasada for Lord Chaitanya. The Lord then ordered Raghunatha to return to Kasi and care for his aged parents. When his parents finally passed away, Raghunatha returned to Jaggani’itha Puri to be with Lord Chaitanya and spent another eight months serving the Lord. One day the Lord asked Raghunatha to proceed to Vrndavana and study Srimad Bhagvatam under the guidance of Rupa and Sanatana Gosvamis, and help them in their mission of fulfilling the Lord's unfinished work of locating all the lost places of Lord Krishna’s pastimes.
On his arrival in Vrndavana, the Gosvamis were very happy to see him and accepted him as their godbrother. Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami 

exhibited all the good qualities of a pure Vaisnava, he were points to criticize, he used to say that since all devotees are engaged in the service of the Lord, he did not mind their faults. Raghunatha became so expert in reciting Srimad Bhagvatarn that he would recite each and every verse in three different ragas or tunes. He regularly recited Bhagvatam in front of Rupa and Sanatana and the assembled Gosvamis, and while doing so he would be overcome by the symptoms of ecstatic love. Tears would flood his eyes, his voice would falter, his hair would stand on end and overcome with ecstasy he would sometimes swoon. It is said that his voice was as sweet as a cuckoo and within no time his fame spread far and wide. Raghunatha's original Srimad Bhagvatam can still be seen at the Bhattaji Mandir in Vrndavana. 

Raghunatha Bhatta ordered disciples to construct the temple of Govindaji. Raghunatha also prepared various ornaments for Govindaji including a flute and shark shaped earnings. Raja Man Singh of Jaipur was an important disciple of Raghunatha Bhatta Gosva‘lmi and it was the Raja who did so much, not only in constructing the Govindaji temple, but also many other temples and ghatas as well. 

Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami was an incarnation of Sri Raga Mafijari, one of the eight intimate maidservants of Srimati Radharani in the spiritual world.

The Godbrother of Lord Chaitanya, Who was sent by Isvara Puri (Lord Chaitanya is spiritual master), to be one of the Lords personal servants at Jagganatha PurI. His sacred body is entombed here at the place Where he did his bhajana, in the garden of the 64 samadhis.
Because he was very big and strong his service to Lord Chaitanya was to walk in front of the Lord when He went to the Jagganatha temple and clear away the crowds with his hands, so that the Lord could proceed with out being touched. KasiSVara also used to distribute prasada after the kirtan to all the devotees. After some years, Lord Chaitanya sent KasiSVara Pandit to Vrndavana to serve the Govindaji Deity of Rupa Gosvami. 
KasiSVara was an incarnation of Lord Krishna's personal servant in the spiritual world called Bhrngara.

Божественный брат Господа Чайтаньи, которого послал Ишвара Пури (Духовный учитель Господа Чайтаньи), быть одним из личных слуг Господа в Джагганатха Пури. Его священное тело погребено здесь, в месте, где он совершал бхаджан, в саду 64 самадхи.
Поскольку он был очень большим и сильным, его служение Господу Чайтаньи заключалось в том, чтобы идти перед Господом, когда Он пошел в храм Джагганатхи и расчищать толпу руками, чтобы Господь мог пройти, чтобы толпа не прикосалась к нему. Кашишвара также был использован для раздачи прасада после киртана для всех преданных. Спустя несколько лет Господь Чайтанья послал Кашишвару Пандита во Вриндаван, чтобы служить Божеству Говиндаджи Рупы Госвами.
Кашишвара был воплощением личного слуги Господа Кришны в духовном мире по имени Брингара.

At this kunda Krishna and Balarama would rest at midday along with their cowherd boyfriends who would massage Their legs while They rested. At this time the cows would drink the cool water of the kunda. This is the largest of the kundas situated in Vrndavana and it is said that both Rupa and Sanatana Gosvami did bhajana on the banks of this kunda. The ghatas of this kunda were built by a princess. Chandharani Kali Sundari of Rajshahi in East Bengal. This kunda is now mostly dry and only fills with water during the rainy season.

На этой кунде Кришна и Баларама отдыхали в полдень вместе с их друзьями-пастушками, которые массажировали Их ноги во время отдыха. В это время коровы пьют прохладную воду кунды. Это самая большая из кунд, расположенных во Вриндаване, и сказано, что и Рупа, и Санатана Госвами совершали бхаджан на берегах этой кунды. Гхаты этой кунды были построены принцессой. Чандхарани Кали Сундари из Раджшахи в Восточной Бенгалии. Эта кунда большую часть времени сухая и наполняется водой только в сезон дождей.

This is the place where Krishna and Balarama ate lunch provided by the wives (janjna patnis) of the sacrificial brahmanas. One day Krishna and Balarama left very early in the morning with their cowherd boyfriends along with their cows and calves for the forest of Vrndavana. After some time the cowherd boys complainted of feeling very hungry because they had come without taking their breakfasts. Lord Krishna then told the cowherd boys that nearby were the houses of the brahmanas the purpose of gaining elevation to the heavenly planets. “Please go to them and ask for charity, but don’t mention My name because I am only a cowherd boy (vais’a). You had better ask in the name of Balarama because he is known as the son of Vasudeva, a KSatriya. These brahmana‘s generally only give charity to other high class brahmanas and do not care for others. They are not Vaisnavas and never chant the holy names of God”.

The cowherd boys then went to the houses of the brahmanas and humbly requested some food that they could eat along with Krishna and Balarama who were tending cows nearby. The cowherd boys said, “Krishna who is non-different from Vishnu, is waiting nearby and you should immediately deliver whatever food you have in stock”. Although the cowherd boys requested charity in the name of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Lord Krishna, the brahmanas did not care to listen to them. Because they were smarta brahmanas and only interested in material benefits through Vedic sacrifice, they could not even appreciate the begging of the Supreme Lord. These caste brahmanas could not understand the actual purpose of the Vedas which is to understand Lord Krishna. Inspite of their advancement of knowledge and rituals, they were ignorant fools and all of their activities were simply a waste of time. 
The cowherd boys being disappointed at being ignored by the brahmanas, returned to Krishna and Balarama and gave the sad news. Surprisingly Krishna asked them to return, but this time to ask the wives of the brahmanas. On returning the cowherd boys approached the wives of the brahmanas, who upon hearing that Krishna and Balarama were asking for food, became anxious to see Them. Being spiritually advanced by thinking of Krishna constantly, they were performing the greatest form of mystic meditation. They did not have to be convinced of the importance of Krishna and Balarama. They had been constantly thinking of how to serve Krishna and Balarama and when they would get the chance to see Them. All the wives of the brahmanas became busy engaged in filling different pots with nice foodstuffs. Because of the performance of the sacrifice, there were so many wonderful preparations that were all very palatable. For a long time the wives had been anxious to see Krishna and Balarama, but as they were leaving home to go and see the Lord, their husbands, fathers, sons and relatives forbade them to go. But the wives did not care, when a devotee is called by the attraction of Krishna he does not care for bodily ties. 
 
The women on entering the forest saw Krishna and Balarama tending the cows along with their boyfriends. Krishna looked so beautiful dressed in garments glittering like gold, He wore a nice garland of forest flowers and had a peacock feather on His head, He was also painted with the minerals found in Vrndavana. He looked exactly like a dancing actor on a theatrical stage. With their very eyes the wives of the brahmanas saw the Supreme Personality of Godhead face to face, within their hearts they began to embrace him to their hearts content. The Lord could understand that they had come to Him despite the protests of their families. They had come to see Him who was their life and soul, and they were perfectly following the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita, that one should surrender to Krishna giving up all other varieties of occupational and religious duties. After thanking the wives of the brahmanas, Krishna and Balarama sat down with their cowherd boyfriends at this same spot and enjoyed the wonderful food sent by the wives of the brahmanas.

Это место, где Кришна и Баларама ели обед, приготовленный женами (janjna patnis) жертвенных брахманов. Однажды Кришна и Баларама ушли очень рано утром со своими друзьями-пастушками, коровами и телятами в лес Вриндавана. Через некоторое время пастушки начали жаловаться, что чувствуют себя очень голодными, потому что они не завтракали. Кришна сказал мальчикам-пастушкам, что поблизости находятся дома брахманов, цель которых достичь райских планет. «Пожалуйста, идите к ним и просите милостыню, но не упоминайте Мое имя, потому что я всего лишь пастушок (вайша). Лучше просите от имени Баларамы, потому что он известен как сын Васудевы, кшатрия. Эти брахманы обычно дают милостыню другим брахманам высокого класса и не заботятся о других. Они не вайшнавы и никогда не повторяют святых имен Бога ».

Мальчики-пастушки отправились в дома брахманов и смиренно попросили еды, которую они могли бы съесть вместе с Кришной и Баларамой, которые пасли коров поблизости. Пастушки сказали: «Кришна, который не отличается от Вишну, ждет рядом, и вы должны немедленно дать все, что у вас есть». Хотя мальчики-пастушки просили милостыню во имя Верховной Личности Бога, Господа Кришны, брахманы не хотели слушать их. Поскольку они были умными брахманами и только интересовались материальными благами через ведическую жертву, они не могли даже оценить милость Верховного Господа.

Эти брахманы не могли понять истинную цель Вед, которой является постижение Господа Кришны. Несмотря на их продвинутое знание и ритуалы, они были невежественными дураками, и вся их деятельность была просто пустой тратой времени.
Пастушки были разочарованы тем, что брахманы их проигнорировали и вернулись к Кришне и Балараме с печальными новостями. К их удивлению Кришна попросил их вернуться, но на этот раз спросить жен брахманов. По возвращении пастушеские мальчики подошли к женам брахманов, которые, услышав, что Кришна и Баларама просили о еде, стали стремиться увидеть Их. Духовно продвинувшись, постоянно размышляя о Кришне, они вели величайшую мистическую медитацию. Им не нужно было быть уверенным в важности Кришны и Баларамы. Они постоянно думали о том, как служить Кришне и Балараме, и когда они получат шанс увидеть Их. Все жены брахманов стали заняты занятием различными горшками с хорошими продуктами питания. Из-за исполнения жертвы было так много замечательных приготовлений, которые были очень вкусными. Долгое время жены стремились увидеть Кришну и Балараму, но когда они уходили из дома, чтобы пойти и увидеть Господа, их мужья, отцы, сыновья и родственники запретили им идти. Но женам было все равно, когда преданного называют притяжением Кришны, он не заботится о телесных связях.
 
Женщины, въезжающие в лес, видели Кришну и Баларама, ухаживая за коровами вместе со своими бойфрендами. Кришна выглядел таким красивым, одетым в одежду, сверкающую как золото. На нем была красивая гирлянда из лесных цветов и на голове у него было павлиньи перья. Он также был нарисован минералами, найденными во Вриндаване. Он был похож на танцующего актера на театральной сцене. Своими глазами жены брахманов увидели Верховную Личность Бога лицом к лицу, в их сердцах они стали обнимать его до их сердца. Господь мог понять, что они пришли к Нему, несмотря на протесты их семей. Они пришли к Нему, кто был их жизнью и душой, и они прекрасно следовали указаниям Бхагавад-Гиты, что нужно предаться Кришне, отказываясь от всех других разновидностей профессиональных и религиозных обязанностей. Поблагодарив жены брахманов, Кришна и Баларама сели вместе с друзьями-пастушками в этом же месте и наслаждались замечательной едой, отправленной женами брахманов.

Here is a very beautiful Deity of Krishna and Balarama with Akrura standing in the middle. This is the sacred place where Akrura stopped to take bath when he was escorting Krishna and Balarama to Mathura at the invitation of King Kamsa. Akrura parked his chariot under the shade of a large tree. Both Krishna and Balarama took bath in the river Yamuna and returned to sit in the chariot. Akrura then took permission from their Lordships and went to take his own bath. 

While standing within the water Akrura suddenly saw Krishna and Balarama, he was very surprised to see them there as he was sure they were sitting on the chariot. Being confused he came out of the water and returned to the chariot and got a shock when he saw Krishna and Balarama still sitting on the chariot as before. He then hurried back to the river and this time he not only saw Krishna and Balarama in the water but also saw they were surrounded by many Demigods, Siddhas, Charanas and Gandharvas. They were all standing before the Lord who was lying down on the serpant Sesa Naga. In other words after again returning to the river he saw that Balarama had changed into Sesa Naga and Krishna had changed into Maha-Vishnu. Out of astonishment Akrura began to offer prayers to the Lord and while Akrura was offering his prayers the Lord suddenly disappeared, therefore Akrura came out of the water and returned to the chariot. 

Krishna and Balarama were still sitting there and Krishna asked Akrura if he had seen something wonderful. Akrura replied that all the wonderful things happening in the world are simply appearing in Your universal form, so when I have seen You, what wonderful thing have I not seen. Akrura continued by saying, “There can not be anything more wonderful than Your transcendental form, when I have seen Your transcendental form, what is there left to see.” After saying this Akrura started the chariot and continued on the road to Mathura. 

Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu also stayed at Akrura Ghata when He visited Vrndavana in 1515. At first the Lord stayed with a brahmanas in Mathura, but due to His great popularity, large crowds would gather from morning to evening just to get a glimpse of the Lord. Therefore Lord Chaitanya felt that it would be better to go to a quite place and so moved to Akrura Ghata. Everyday Lord Chaitanya would visit the holy places of Vrndavana and in the evening return to Akrura Ghata. The Yamuna river used to flow past this place but since many years the river bed has shifted away and is now situated about one kilometer to the West.

Здесь находится очень красивое Божество Кришны и Баларама с Акрурой, стоящим посередине. Это священное место, где Акрура остановился, чтобы принять ванну, когда он сопровождал Кришну и Балараму в Матхуру по приглашению короля Камсы. Акрура припарковал свою колесницу под тенью большого дерева. Кришна и Баларама приняли омовение в реке Ямуне и вернулись в колесницу. Затем Акрура получил разрешение принять омовение.

Стоя в воде, Акрура внезапно увидел Кришну и Балараму, он был очень удивлен, увидев их там, поскольку он был уверен, что они сидят на колеснице. Смутившись, он вышел из воды и вернулся на колесницу и был шоккирован, когда увидел, что Кришна и Баларама все еще сидят на колеснице, как и раньше. Затем он поспешил обратно к реке, и на этот раз он не только видел Кришну и Балараму в воде, но также видел, что их окружали полубоги, сиддхи, чараны и гандхарвы. Все они стояли перед Господом, который лежал на Ананта-шеше. Другими словами, снова вернувшись к реке, он увидел, что Баларама превратился в Ананта-шешу, а Кришна превратился в Маха-Вишну. Из изумления Акрура начал молиться Господу, и, когда Акрура предлагал свои молитвы, Господь внезапно исчез, поэтому Акрура вышел из воды и вернулся на колесницу.

Кришна и Баларама все еще сидели, и Кришна спросил Акруру, видел ли он что-то замечательное. Акрура ответил, что все чудесные вещи, происходящие в мире, просто появляются в вашей Вселенской форме. Поэтому, когда я увидел Тебя, какие чудесные явления мне еще нужны. Акрура продолжал говорить: «Не может быть ничего более прекрасного, чем Твоя трансцендентная форма, когда я увижу Твою трансцендентную форму, что мне еще осталось увидеть». После этого Акрура начал движение в колеснице и продолжил путь в Матхуру.

Господь Чайтанья Махапрабху также остался в Акруре Гата, когда посетил Вриндаван в 1515 году. Сначала Господь остался с брахманами в Матхуре, но из-за его большой популярности большие толпы собирались с утра до вечера, чтобы получить представление о Господе. Поэтому Господь Чайтанья почувствовал, что было бы лучше отправиться на место и так переехать в Акруру Гату. Каждый день Господь Чайтанья посещал святые места Вриндавана, а вечером возвращался в Акруру Гату. Река Ямуна проходила мимо этого места, но с тех пор русло реки поменялось и теперь находится примерно в одном километре от запада.

One of the most famous sweetshop-cafes in Vrindavana. Here you can try the best traditional exotic sweets such as rajbhogs or spesial Vrindavan treats called perra, which you can get only in Braja. It`s well known that every sweetshop keeper`s family in India has their own secret recipe for every traditional dish, so wherever you try any sweet, you`ll get very special unique experience.

Один из самых известных кафе-магазинчиков сладостей во Вриндаване. Здесь вы можете попробовать лучшие традиционные экзотические сладости, такие как раджбхоги, или специальное лакомство Вриндавана, которое называется перра, его вы можете попробовать только во Врадже. Хорошо известно, что у каждого владельца магазина сластей в Индии есть свой собственный тайный семейный рецепт для каждого традиционного блюда, поэтому, где бы вы ни попробовали любую сладость, вы всегда получите особый, уникальный опыт.
Nice and fancy place to stay and eat  by ISKCON.
Приятное и довольно роскошное место, где можно остановиться и покушать. Приурочено к МОСК (ISKCON)
One of ISKCON`s places to stay.
Одно из мест под знаменем МОСК (ISKCON), где можно остановиться.
One of the best places to have a lunch not far from many important spots.
Одно из лучших мест, где можно покушать, в разумной удаленности от многих важных точек паломничества.
Одно из уличных кафе Вриндавана с разнообразной кухней.
Perhaps, the most popular place among tourists.
Пожалуй, самое популярное среди туристов заведение.
4 stars hotel, average bill is about 1000 - 1500 rs.
4х звездочный отель со средним чеком в районе 1000 - 1500 рупий.

Set 3 km from Vrindavan train station, this unpretentious hotel in a streamlined building with a glass facade is a 10-minute walk from the grand Prem Mandir Vrindavan Hindu temple.
Down-to-earth rooms offer sitting areas, satellite TV and Wi-Fi access. Suites come with separate living areas. Room service is offered.
There's a casual vegetarian restaurant and a doctor-on-call. Parking is available.

Этот отель расположен в 3 км от железнодорожного вокзала Вриндавана, в элегантном здании со стеклянным фасадом, в 10 минутах ходьбы от величественного индуистского храма Прем Мандир.
В просторных номерах есть гостиные зоны, спутниковое телевидение и Wi-Fi. В люксах есть отдельные гостиные. Предоставляются услуги обслуживания номеров.
В отеле есть неформальный вегетарианский ресторан и врач по вызову. Имеется парковка.
One of most popular touristic food places.
Одно из самых популярных среди туристов заведений.

Almst unknown place. Here you will be offered to try dishes of national cuisine at affordable price and you will be pleased with the taste and quality of food. Very important feature is the cordial staff, ready to save the Western man's receptors and add a little less chili in your dish if you ask for it.

Малоизвестное место. Здесь вам предложат блюда национальной кухни по приемлемой цене и порадуют вкусом и качеством пищи. Очень важной особенностью является радушный персонал, готовый пожалеть рецепторы западного человека и добавить поменьше чили, если вы попросите об этом.
The most popular Govardhan sweet shop.
Самый популярный магазин сладостей на Говардхане.
Hotel Wingston, average bill is about 2000 - 2500 rs.
Отель Вингстон, средний чек около 2000 - 2500 рупий.

If arriving by air, then the economical but also the safest way to reach Vrindvan is to go to New Delhi Railway Station, take a train to Mathura, then an auto-rickshaw to the place where you`re going to stay in Vrindavan.
The very fast and modern Delhi Airport Express metro connects Terminal 3 with New Delhi Railway Station. 
 
Take an air-conditioned bus from outside the terminal to New Delhi train station (about Rs70) or a prepaid taxi to the front entrance of New Delhi railway station (about Rs300).
By Train from Delhi: Take one of the frequent trains from New Delhi to Mathura Junction. It is definitely better to get a reservation, and practical too. If you can, book a seat in advance, but if you don't already have a reservation and just turn up at New Delhi Station, this should help:
At New Delhi station there's a clearly marked 'Enquiry' counter to the right of the main front entrance (the Pahar Ganj side). Ask the time and names of the next *two* express trains to Mathura Junction. Get a reservation form from beside the "Current Booking" counter just to the right of the Enquiries. Fill up the form and get your ticket there at the Current Booking counter. This almost always works, because many people get on at Mathura, so there are spare berths/seats from Delhi to Mathura. You'd be unlucky not to get a reservation in one of the next two trains in one of the classes.
Otherwise you need to find an International Tourist Bureau and they will help you to find the best way to get to Mathura and give you all information, which you need. It`s not so easy to find if it is your first time, you can ask someone but beware of people who will try to take you to any other unofficial places where they will claim you can buy a ticket. To find the Bureau you shall turn right just at the entrance to the first platform, you will see the stairs and the red sign on the top International Tourist Bureau. Go up and follow the signs, you will find it easily. Take an electronic token and wait for your turn.
From outside Mathura Junction railway station take a three-wheeled auto-rickshaw to Vrindavan for Rs 150-300 without sharing, directly to the place.
However, it's just Rs 20 from Mathura Station to the bus station in Vrindavan if you're prepared to take a shared autorickshaw (sharing with many). From the bus stand in Vrindavan ask directions to the place you need.
Alternatively, from outside Mathura Junction railway station take a public bus all the way to Vrindavan bus station (Rs20). 
By Taxi from Delhi: Arrangements can be made outside Delhi Airport or Delhi Railway Stations for a taxi to Vrindavan for about Rs 6000. The 130 km drive takes about three-four hours. When bartering for a taxi, be sure to be clear that all road toll taxes are included in the price.

Если вы прибываете по воздуху, то самым простым и безопасным способом добраться до Вриндавана будет отправиться поездом с железнодорожной станции Нью-Дели до Матхуры, а затем взять авто-рикшу до места, где вы планируете остановиться во Вриндаване. Очень быстрый и современный поезд “Аэропорт Дели Экспресс метро” соединять третий терминал аэропорта с железнодорожной станцией Нью-Дели. 
Или сядьте на кондиционируемый автобус от терминала до станции (около 70 рупий) , или предоплаченное такси прямо до главного входа станции Нью-Дели (около 300 рупий). Кабинка предоплаченного такси находится на выходе из аэропорта. 
Поездом из Дели: сядьте на один из частых поездов, идущих от Дели до станции Матхура Джанкшн. Лучше всего, если у вас есть предварительное бронирование, так что, если у вас есть возможность, постарайтесь забронировать билеты заранее, это будет очень практично. Но если вы просто приехали на станцию Нью-Дели, то это должно помочь:
На станции, справа от главного входа, со стороны Пахарганж, есть четко обозначенное окошко “Enquiry”. Узнайте время отправления и названия ближайших двух поездов до Матхуры и получите форму бронирования у окошка “Current Booking”, справа от окошка справок. Заполните форму и дале купите билет в окошке текущего бронирования. Это почти всегда работает, потому что очень большой поток людей направляется в Матхуру и места обеспечены почти всегда. Очень маловероятно, чтобы вам не повезло попасть хотя бы на один из этих двух поездов, в любой класс. 
В противном случае вам следует найти Интернациональное Туристическое Бюро и там вам помогут найти наилучший способ добраться до Матхуры и дадут всю необходимую информацию, которая вам понадобится. Не так уж просто найти его, если это ваш первый раз, вы можете спросить кого-то, но будьте осторожны с людьми, которые могут попробовать увести вас в какое-нибудь неофициальное место, где, как они будут утверждать, вы можете купить билет. И так, чтобы добраться до туда, вам нужно повернуть направо прямо у входа на первую платформу, там вы увидите лестницу и над нею будет красный знак Интернациональное Туристическое Бюро. Поднимайтесь по лестнице и следуйте знакам, вы легко найдете свою цель. Возьмите талончик электронной очереди и дожидайтесь, пока вас позовут. 
Выйдя со станции в Матхуре вы можете взять трехколесную авторикшу до Вриндавана за 150-300 рупий (торгуйтесь), которая довезет вас прямо до места, которое вы назовете. 
Однако, от Матхуры до автобусной станции Вриндавана рикша будет стоить всегда 20 рупий, если вы готовы ехать на общественной рикше (когда набирается сразу несколько человек). Уже ао Вриндаване от автобусной станции вы можете поймать рикшу до нужного вам места. 
Как альтернатива рикше, есть автобус, который ходит от ж/д станции Матхура Джанкшн до Вриндавана, такой автобус стоит около 20 рупий. 
Можно добраться на такси от Дели: Вы можете организовать себе такси до Вриндавана за пределами аэропорта Дели, или ж/д станции Нью-Дели. Это будет стоить около 6000 рупий, дорога в 130 километров займет около 3-4 часов. Убедитесь, что в оговоренную сумму будут включены все налоги и дорожные сборы.

First get to Mathura (see how to ge to Vrindavan). Then a rickshaw from the Railway Station to 'New Bus Stand' (Rs 10). Then by bus to Govardhan (40+ minutes journey, about Rs 25). Get off at Govardhan bus station.
You can also take a rickshaw directly from Mathura railway station to the place you need in Govardhan, it will cost you about Rs 200-300.

Сначала нужно добраться до Матхуры (смотрите направление до Вриндавана). Затем возьмите Рикшу от ж/д станции до Автобусной станации 'New Bus Stand', около 10 рупий. Затем сядьте на автобус до Говардхана. Путешествие займет около 40 минут и обойдется вам примерно в 25 рупий.
Вы так же можете взять индивидуальную рикшу непосредственно от железнодорожной станции Матхуры до нужного вам места на Говардхане, такая поездка обойдется вам в 200-300 рупий.

If arriving in Kolkata (formerly Calcutta) by air:
a) By Taxi: Bartering with one of the many taxi drivers approaching you outside should, at best, get Rs 1,700 per taxi to Nabadwip or Rs2,000 for a Tata Sumo (Indian larger 'Land-Cruiser' style vehicle). It's a 3+ hour drive, and can be bumpy in parts.
b) By Train: First, take a pre-paid taxi to the station. Inside each of the domestic and international airport buildings by the exit is a Prepaid Taxi Booth. Prepay a taxi to Howrah Railway Station (now over Rs 200 and 1 to 2 hours) and take a train 
c) By Express Bus: Take a rickshaw to 'Ek Number Gate' (Rs 20) then by bus to Nabadwip.

From Howrah Station: 'Good' local trains leave Howrah Railway Station (Kolkata's main station) at 6.35 a.m., 7.55 a.m., 12.20 p.m., 2.35 p.m., 5 p.m., 6 p.m. all these are known as the 'Katwa Local.' They each take over 3 hours. On each of these trains is a coach reserved for ladies.
All unreserved train tickets to Nabadwip Dham from Howrah Station are available in the booking hall to the right on entering the main entrance of the station. Go to the left hand section of windows there (marked 'Eastern Railways Suburban Booking'). Current price: Rs 24 (RS32 Express). Ladies can purchase all 2nd class tickets from the 'Ladies Only' window.
From Shaeldah Station (Kolkata's 2nd railway station): 
Tickets for all trains are available to the left on entering the Shaeldah Station building, from window 9 and adjacent windows. This express ticket is about Rs 40.

Arriving at Nabadwip Station, many rickshaws are available. They will take you to the place you`re going to stay at.
N.B. A good tip to bargaining is to have the amount you are ready to spend in your hand and show it to the rickshaw man when setting the price and perhaps say that you don't have any more, it reduces bargaining to almost zero.
iii) By bus:
Express buses are generally fast but having anything more than hand baggage can be a problem. They all pick up passengers at 'Ek Number Gate' near the airport, a 10 minute walk or Rs 20 rickshaw ride from the Airport, and at 'Ultadanga' – each is at the opposite end of V.I.P. Road. Take any express bus (approx. Rs 40) that stops at Krishnanagar (commonly: the buses going to Bhorampur or Malda). Get down at 'Krishnanagar High Road' on the by-pass of Krishnanagar, most buses stop here for refreshments. Walk 500 metres onwards and wait at the first turning left for a local bus going to Bablari (Rs 8), or any Express Bus going to 'Nabadwip Rail Gate.'
Here you can take a rickshaw to the place.

Если вы прибыли в Калькутту по воздуху:
A) Такси: Можно договориться с одним из многих таксистов, которых вы увидите выйдя из аэропорта, это будет стоить вам от 1700 до 2000 рупий. Дорога займет более трех часов, и, возможно, вас немного потрясет в пути.
Б) На поезде: сначала возьмите ​​предоплаченное такси до железнодорожной станции. На выходе из каждого терминала международного аэропорта находятся будки заказа такси с предоплатой. оплатите  такси до железнодорожного вокзала Howrah (около 200-300 рупий) и садитесь на поезд.
C) Экспресс-автобус: возьмите рикшу в «Ek Number Gate» (Rs 20), затем сядьте на автобус до Навадвипа.

От станции Howrah: «Хорошие» местные поезда отправляются со станции ​​Howrah (главная станция Калькутты) в 6:35 , 7:55, 12:20 , 2:35 , 5 часов, 6 часов. Все они будут называться «Katwa Local». Каждый из них идет примерно 3 часа. На каждом из этих поездов есть вагон, предназначенный только для женщин.
Все незарезервированные железнодорожные билеты до Навадвипа от станции Howrah доступны в зале для бронирования справа от главного входа на станции. Идите к окошкам слева там обозначено «Пригородное бронирование Восточной железной дороги». Цена билета около 24 рупий (32 за экспресс). Дамы могут приобрести все билеты второго класса из окна «Только для женщин».
От станции Shaeldah, (2-й железнодорожный вокзал Калькутты):
Билеты на все поезда доступны слева при входе в здание станции Shaeldah, из окна 9 и соседних окон. Цена на этот экспресс-билет составляет около 40 рупий.

Прибывая на станцию ​​Набадвип, можно взять любую из множества рикш. Они отвезут вас в место, где вы собираетесь остановится.
Нотабене: Лучше всего заранее договариваться с рикшей о цене проезда, показать ему обозначенную сумму. Если вы скажете, что больше денег у вас просто нет, это поможет свести торги к нулю.
Автобусом:
Экспресс-автобусы обычно идут довольно быстро, но вопрос с перевозкой багажа может быть проблемой. Они все подбирают пассажиров в «Ek Number Gate» рядом с аэропортом, (в 10 минутах ходьбы от аэропорта, или на рикше от 20 рупий), и в «Ultadanga» - все они стоят на противоположном конце V.I.P. дорожки. Садитесь на любой экспресс-автобус (около 40 рупий), который останавливается в Кришнанагаре (обычно: автобусы отправляются в Борампур или Мальду). Сходите на остановке «Кришнанагарская Скоростная Дорога», эта дорога идет вокруг Кришнанагара, большинство автобусов останавливаются здесь, чтобы перекусить. Пройдите 500 метров вперед до первого поворота налево, где останавливается местный автобус, идущий в Баблари (8 рупий), или любой экспресс-автобус, идущий на «Железные ворота Набадвипа».
Здесь вы можете взять рикшу до нужного вам места.

Puri is an 11 hour train journey from Kolkata. Two overnight trains leave each evening and there's a very good daily train, the Howrah Puri Duronto Express each afternoon at 2:25pm. 
There are is a good express train, the 'Dhauli Express', departing daily from Howrah at 6 a.m. going to Puri.
Many, many trains go to Puri and to nearby Bhubaneshwar from where you change to a local train to Puri or take a bus or taxi (1+ hours). See indiarailinfo.com for fuller information.
Express trains also go from Delhi to Puri and vice versa. They take about 36 hours. 
From Puri Railway Station take a rickshaw to the place you are going to stay at.

Пури находится в 11 часах езды на поезде от Калькутты. Два ночных поезда отправляются каждый вечер, и каждый день в 2:25 отходит очень хороший ежедневный поезд, Howrah Puri Duronto Express.
Есть хороший экспресс, «Дхаули Экспресс», отправляющийся ежедневно из Хавра в 6 утра в Пури.
Много, очень много поездов отправляются в Пури и в соседний Бхубанешвар, где вы можете пересесть на местный поезд в Пури, или автобус, а так же взять такси (около часа в дороге). См. Indiarailinfo.com для получения более полной информации.
Экспресс-поезда также отправляются из Дели в Пури и наоборот. Они идут около 36 часов.
От железнодорожного вокзала в Пури возьмите рикшу до того места, где вы собираетесь остановиться.

Ekachakra, the holy Appearance Place of Sri Nityananda Prabhu, is also quite out of the way but there is a daily bus leaving from outside Nabadwip Railway Station at 4am daily. It takes about 6 hours to reach Birchadrapur. Once there, ask for 'Nityananda Bari' (Appearance Place). 
A direct bus goes from Birchandrapur back to Nabadwip at 11:15am daily.
By train from Kolkata: Express trains from Kolkata to Rampurhat. A taxi from Rampurhat to Birchandrapur should cost no more than Rs400 and takes about 45 minutes. Autorickshaw should be no more than Rs300.
Alternatively can take a shared auto to the famous local tourist spot, Tarapit. Then a further shared auto for Rs20 each to Birchandrapur.

Экачакра, святое место явления Шри Нитьянанды Прабху, имеет очень неудобное расположение, но ежедневно в 4 утра туда выезжают автобусы со стороны железнодорожного вокзала Набадвип. До Бирчадрапура на дорогу требуется около 6 часов. По прибытию туда, спросите как добраться до «Нитьянанда Бари».
Прямой автобус отправляется из Бирхандрапура обратно в Набадвип в 11:15 ежедневно.
На поезде из Калькутты: экспресс поездом из Калькутты в Рампурхат, затем такси от Рампурхата до Бирхандрапура, которое должно стоить не более 400 рупий и дорога занимает около 45 минут. Авторикша должна стоить не более 300 рупий.
В качестве альтернативы можно взять общее авто до известного туристического места Тарапитt. Затем еще одино общее авто до Бирчадрапура за 20 рупий с человека.

As per Hindu legend, the temple is associated with the times of Ramayana. Raghunathji (Rama, an avatar of Vishnu) is believed to performed penance at this place to relieve himself off the curse committed by killing Ravana, a Brahmin demon king.
Согласно индуистской легенде, храм связан со временами Рамаяны. Рагунатхи (Рама, аватар Вишну), место совершения жертвоприношения, чтобы освободиться от проклятия, совершенного убийством Равана

Raghunathji Temple (also called Tirukantamenum Kadi Nagar) in Devprayag, a pilgrimage town in Tehri Garhwal district in Himalayas in the North Indian state of Uttarakhand, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. It is located 73 km from Rishikesh on the Rishikesh - Badrinath highway.
